steps.title: Use calories as the input
steps.content: The question says $x$ food calories is equivalent to $k$ kilojoules, so use calories as the input and kilojoules as the output. Two table rows are $(4.0,16.7)$ and $(9.0,37.7)$.

steps.title: Find the conversion rate
steps.content: Compute the rate of change in kilojoules per food calorie: $$\frac{37.7-16.7}{9.0-4.0}=\frac{21}{5}=4.2$$ Thus $k$ is $4.2$ times $x$, so the relationship is $k=4.2x$. The answer is B.
